violations.text: No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Chicken was placed on bottom shelf. Proper storage guidance document provided. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026

All foods were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026
Cloths were used on top of food. PIC switched to plastic wrap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the shelving of refrigeration units and the slider door have an accumulation of food residue and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Chicken was stored above beef. Chicken was relocated. Proper storage reviewed with PIC. Another guidance document was provided. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/12/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the shelving in the walk in have an accumulation of dust and food residue. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/12/2025

There was no evidence that employees are required to report to the person in charge information about their health (illnesses, symptoms) as they relate to diseases that are transmissible through food. Paperwork provided. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2025
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Paperwork provided. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2025

Raw animal foods (beef) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (vegetables) in the reach-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer, knives) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Proper sanitizing procedures reviewed with PIC. Knives and holder taken to 3-bay. Slicer to not be used before sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2025

Various foods were observed held above 41 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was out of temperature for less than 4 hours. Food relocated to walk-in to rapidly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2025

Thermometers are lacking in the refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2025
The sliding door refrigerator was observed unable to recover temperature in a timely manner. Unit taken out of service. Unit to be serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the various shelving and ice machine have an accumulation of dust, food residue, and other debris. Shelves and ice machine to be cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2024

The floor in the basement area has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris, rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2024

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Inspector demonstrated how to create a proper sanitizer using bleach. PIC to instruct all staff on how to do so. Establishment to use test strips on solution prior to using. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/14/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/14/2024

Dishes, miscellaneous items, and utensils were observed in the hand-washing sink at the bar. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Hand sink to be easily accessible/usable at all times. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024

Raw animal foods (pork/fish) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (vegetables) in the walk-in refrigerator and sliding door refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Food was relocated. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024

Egg rolls were observed held at 130-132 degrees Fahrenheit on the hot hold line. Egg rolls were observed stored on a grease drip rack on top of a metal pan. Inspector discussed methods for keeping food above 135 degrees. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Establishment to ensure food is maintained above 135 degrees. Egg rolls were removed by PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024

Various Foods were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Container was emptied and filled with proper sanitizing solution ppm. Proper sanitizing solution reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024
Glue fly traps were observed in the down stairs dry storage area. Glue fly traps were removed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024
The condensate from the prep unit refrigeration unit is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2024

Raw animal foods (such as beef) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as lemon water) in the refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as lemon water during storage. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/01/2023
Multiple containers of food were observed uncovered in the refrigerator. Food must be covered to protect contamination from the premises. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/18/2023

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Raw chicken was observed stored in the same container as raw pork. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Chicken was removed from the container at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Can opener was cleaned during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2023

Multiple containers of food were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Chicken fingers were observed cooling in containers greater than four inches deep on the counter. Chicken fingers were placed in a shallow pan and relocated to the walk-in cooler during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2023

Boxes of food were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. The person in charge relocated the food off the floor during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2023
Multiple containers of food were observed uncovered in cold-hold units. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2023

Wet wiping cloths are not stored in a chemical sanitizer between uses. Wet wiping cloths must be stored in a chemical sanitizer at the appropriate concentration. Wet wiping cloth was relocated to the sanitizing bucket during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2023

The cutting boards in the kitchen and basement are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2023

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris, especially under the fryolator and behind equipment. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/02/2023

Employees' toothpaste was observed stored on the mop sink. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. Toothpaste was relocated during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2023
